Deep Structural Causal Model (DSCM)

Source: R/causalDeepNet.R
dscm.Rd

Fits a Deep Structural Causal Model following the tutorial in deep_structural _causalML_DSCMs.ipynb. The model contains: (1) a treatment mechanism \(p(T \mid X)\) and (2) an outcome mechanism with latent abduction \(q(Z \mid X, T, Y)\) and potential-outcome heads \(Y(0), Y(1)\).

Usage

dscm(
  X,
  treatment,
  y,
  y0_true = NULL,
  y1_true = NULL,
  hidden_dim = 256L,
  treatment_hidden_dim = 128L,
  latent_dim = 16L,
  dropout = 0.1,
  num_epochs = 300L,
  batch_size = 256L,
  learning_rate = 2e-04,
  weight_decay = 1e-05,
  val_frac = 0.15,
  kl_warmup_epochs = 80L,
  max_beta = 0.25,
  lambda_oracle = 0,
  patience = 60L,
  max_grad_norm = 5,
  verbose = TRUE,
  device = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

X

Covariate matrix or data.frame.

treatment

Binary treatment vector (0/1).

y

Outcome vector.

y0_true

Optional true \(Y(0)\) for oracle supervision (e.g., IHDP simulations).

y1_true

Optional true \(Y(1)\) for oracle supervision (e.g., IHDP simulations).

hidden_dim

Hidden size for outcome network (default 256).

treatment_hidden_dim

Hidden size for treatment network (default 128).

latent_dim

Latent noise dimension for abduction (default 16).

dropout

Dropout rate (default 0.1).

num_epochs

Number of epochs (default 300).

batch_size

Batch size (default 256).

learning_rate

AdamW learning rate (default 2e-4).

weight_decay

AdamW weight decay (default 1e-5).

val_frac

Validation fraction (default 0.15).

kl_warmup_epochs

Number of KL warmup epochs (default 80).

max_beta

Maximum KL weight after warmup (default 0.25).

lambda_oracle

Oracle supervision weight (default 0).

patience

Early stopping patience on validation objective (default 60).

max_grad_norm

Gradient clipping norm (default 5).

verbose

If TRUE, print progress every 25 epochs.

device

Device string: "cuda", "cpu", or NULL.

...

Ignored.

Value

Object of class dscm.

Details

Counterfactual inference follows abduction-action-prediction (AAP): infer latent noise from factual data, intervene on treatment, then decode counterfactual outcomes.

See also

RCausalML-package

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# dscm(...)
} # }
